The **3 Seventh Avenue Express**[[3]](#cite_note-timetable-3) is a [rapid transit](/wiki/Rapid_transit "Rapid transit") service in the [A Division](/wiki/A_Division_(New_York_City_Subway) "A Division (New York City Subway)") of the [New York City Subway](/wiki/New_York_City_Subway "New York City Subway"). Its route emblem, or "bullet", is colored red since it uses the [IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line](/wiki/IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line "IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line") through most of [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The 3 operates 24 hours daily, although service patterns vary based on the time of day. Daytime service operates between [148th Street](/wiki/Harlem%E2%80%93148th_Street_(IRT_Lenox_Avenue_Line) "Harlem–148th Street (IRT Lenox Avenue Line)") in [Harlem, Manhattan](/wiki/Harlem,_Manhattan "Harlem, Manhattan") and [New Lots Avenue](/wiki/New_Lots_Avenue_(IRT_New_Lots_Line) "New Lots Avenue (IRT New Lots Line)") in [East New York, Brooklyn](/wiki/East_New_York,_Brooklyn "East New York, Brooklyn"), making express stops in Manhattan (between [96th](/wiki/96th_Street_(IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line) "96th Street (IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line)") and [Chambers Streets](/wiki/Chambers_Street_(IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line) "Chambers Street (IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line)")) and all stops in [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"). Overnight service [short turns](/wiki/Short_turn "Short turn") at [34th Street–Penn Station](/wiki/34th_Street%E2%80%93Penn_Station_(IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line) "34th Street–Penn Station (IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line)") in [Midtown Manhattan](/wiki/Midtown_Manhattan "Midtown Manhattan") and does not operate to or from New Lots Avenue.

The 3 train formerly ran to [City Hall](/wiki/City_Hall_(IRT_Lexington_Avenue_Line) "City Hall (IRT Lexington Avenue Line)") or [South Ferry](/wiki/South_Ferry_loops "South Ferry loops") in [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan"), and was later rerouted to [Flatbush Avenue–Brooklyn College](/wiki/Flatbush_Avenue%E2%80%93Brooklyn_College_(IRT_Nostrand_Avenue_Line) "Flatbush Avenue–Brooklyn College (IRT Nostrand Avenue Line)") in [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"). In 1983, it was rerouted to New Lots Avenue.

## Service history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3_(New_York_City_Subway_service)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Service history")]

### Early history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3_(New_York_City_Subway_service)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early history")]

On November 23, 1904, the [IRT Lenox Avenue Line](/wiki/IRT_Lenox_Avenue_Line "IRT Lenox Avenue Line") opened between 96th Street and 145th Street. Service ran between [145th Street](/wiki/145th_Street_(IRT_Lenox_Avenue_Line) "145th Street (IRT Lenox Avenue Line)") and [City Hall](/wiki/City_Hall_(IRT_Lexington_Avenue_Line) "City Hall (IRT Lexington Avenue Line)"), making all stops.[[5]](#cite_note-erictb.info-5)[*[better source needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*]

On July 1, 1918, the entire [IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line](/wiki/IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line "IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line") was completed. Lenox Avenue service from 145th Street was rerouted south of 42nd Street from the [IRT Lexington Avenue Line](/wiki/IRT_Lexington_Avenue_Line "IRT Lexington Avenue Line") to this new line, making all stops to [South Ferry](/wiki/South_Ferry_(IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line) "South Ferry (IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line)").[[5]](#cite_note-erictb.info-5)[*[better source needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*]

As of 1934, service operated between 145th Street and South Ferry except late nights, when service operated between 145th Street and 96th Street, making local stops.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The IRT routes were given numbered designations with the introduction of ["R-type" rolling stock](/wiki/List_of_New_York_City_Subway_R-type_contracts "List of New York City Subway R-type contracts"), which contained [rollsign curtains](/wiki/Rollsign "Rollsign") with numbered designations for each service. The first such fleet, the [R12](/wiki/R12_(New_York_City_Subway_car) "R12 (New York City Subway car)"), was put into service in 1948.[[6]](#cite_note-6) The Seventh Avenue–Lenox route became known as the 3.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### Under the New York City Transit Authority

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3_(New_York_City_Subway_service)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Under the New York City Transit Authority")]

On January 3, 1955, late night two-car shuttle service between 145th Street and 96th Street, between 12:12 and 6:57 a.m. was discontinued due to low ridership. As a result, 145th Street was closed overnight. In addition, some 3 trains started running express in Manhattan during rush hours.[[8]](#cite_note-8) These trains were extended to Flatbush Avenue; a few layups and put-ins ran to New Lots Avenue.[[5]](#cite_note-erictb.info-5) However, from December 20, 1957, 3 trains were rerouted to New Lots Avenue during rush hours. On February 6, 1959, all trains except late nights made express stops in Manhattan as part of the "West Side Improvement" and ran to Flatbush Avenue.[[5]](#cite_note-erictb.info-5)

Starting April 8, 1960, 3 trains were rerouted from Flatbush Avenue to New Lots Avenue, and weekday evening service between 9 p.m. and 1 a.m. was cut to a shuttle between 145th Street and 135th Street. Before the change, 3 trains had run between 145th Street and Flatbush Avenue between about 5:30 a.m. and 12:45 a.m. on weekdays and Saturdays and from about 7:15 a.m. to 12:45 a.m. on Sundays. This service had been supplemented by additional service to and from New Lots Avenue.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

However, on April 18, 1965, 3 service started to run to Flatbush Avenue again.[[5]](#cite_note-erictb.info-5) On October 17, 1965, weekend evening service was also cut to a shuttle between 145th Street and 135th Street.

On May 13, 1968, trains were extended to the newly completed [148th Street–Lenox Terminal](/wiki/Harlem%E2%80%93148th_Street_station "Harlem–148th Street station").[[11]](#cite_note-NYTImes-148LenoxOpen-1968-11) Later that year, on November 12, late night shuttle service was implemented between 148th Street and 135th Street.[[12]](#cite_note-12) On May 23, 1976, the current practice of starting Sunday service late (9 or 10 a.m.) began.[[5]](#cite_note-erictb.info-5)

On July 10, 1983, the [2](/wiki/2_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "2 (New York City Subway service)") and 3 trains swapped terminals in Brooklyn, with 2 trains terminating at Flatbush Avenue and 3 trains terminating at New Lots Avenue. These changes were made to reduce non-revenue subway car mileage, to provide a dedicated fleet for each service, and to provide an easily accessible inspection yard for each service. The change allowed the 2 to be dedicated to [239th Street Yard](/wiki/239th_Street_Yard "239th Street Yard") and allowed the 3 to be assigned to [Livonia Yard](/wiki/Livonia_Yard "Livonia Yard"). With the rerouting of 3 trains, train lengths along the New Lots Line were reduced from 10 cars to 9 cars, within acceptable crowding levels, and train lengths along the Nostrand Avenue Line were increased from 9 to 10 cars, reducing crowding.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Beginning on August 5, 1990, late-night shuttles between 148th Street and 135th Street were discontinued and replaced by shuttle buses. On September 4, 1994, late-night shuttles between 148th Street and 135th Street were resumed, but were discontinued again on September 10, 1995.[[15]](#cite_note-15) This was a cost-saving measure due to low ridership.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

In January 1991, a reduction of service along the Eastern Parkway corridor to remove excess capacity was proposed. Weekend daytime 3 service would be rerouted to originate and terminate at [South Ferry](/wiki/South_Ferry_(IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line) "South Ferry (IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line)") in Manhattan instead of [New Lots Avenue](/wiki/New_Lots_Avenue_(IRT_New_Lots_Line) "New Lots Avenue (IRT New Lots Line)") in Brooklyn. Service between [Chambers Street](/wiki/Chambers_Street_(IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line) "Chambers Street (IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line)") and [Franklin Avenue](/wiki/Franklin_Avenue_(IRT_Eastern_Parkway_Line) "Franklin Avenue (IRT Eastern Parkway Line)") would be provided by [2](/wiki/2_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "2 (New York City Subway service)") trains; service between Franklin Avenue and New Lots Avenue would be provided by extended [4](/wiki/4_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "4 (New York City Subway service)") trains. This service change would have been implemented in July 1991, pending approval from the MTA board.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

From March 2[[18]](#cite_note-18) to October 12, 1998, the [IRT Lenox Avenue Line](/wiki/IRT_Lenox_Avenue_Line "IRT Lenox Avenue Line") was rehabilitated. Most 3 service was rerouted to [137th Street–City College](/wiki/137th_Street%E2%80%93City_College_(IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line) "137th Street–City College (IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line)").[[19]](#cite_note-19) From October 4, 1999, to August 3, 2001, Limited AM rush hour 3 trains originated at [137th Street – City College](/wiki/137th_Street_%E2%80%93_City_College_(IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line) "137th Street – City College (IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line)") instead of 148th Street; these trains ran local to 96th Street before switching to the express track. From August 6, 2001,[[20]](#cite_note-20) to April 25, 2003, limited AM rush hour 3 trains originated at [East 180th Street](/wiki/East_180th_Street_(IRT_White_Plains_Road_Line) "East 180th Street (IRT White Plains Road Line)") in the Bronx instead of 148th Street.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

### Recent history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3_(New_York_City_Subway_service)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Recent history")]

After the [September 11, 2001](/wiki/September_11_attacks "September 11 attacks") attacks, 3 service was initially split in two sections, with the northern section operating between 148th and 135th Streets and the southern section operating between Utica Avenue and New Lots Avenue, but service in Brooklyn was replaced by 4 trains on the evening of September 12, leaving the 3 to operate only as a shuttle between 148th and 135th Streets. On September 17, service was restored along the full route and made all stops in Manhattan, with trains skipping Franklin Street, Chambers Street and Park Place; Franklin Street reopened on September 18. On September 19, service was cut back from New Lots Avenue to 14th Street in Manhattan; 3 trains made express stops between 96th and 14th Streets, while the 1 replaced the 3 in Brooklyn.[[22]](#cite_note-22) Also on this date, 3 trains began to permanently use consists of 10-car trains as a result of equipment sharing between Livonia Yard (the maintenance facility in Brooklyn where subway cars assigned to the 3 are based from) and 240th Street Yard (the maintenance facility in the Bronx where subway cars assigned to the 1 are based from).[[23]](#cite_note-23) Normal 3 service was restored on September 15, 2002.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

On July 27, 2008, late night 3 service was restored, operating express between 148th Street and Times Square–42nd Street. In addition, late morning weekday service was increased from running every 6 to 8 minutes to running every 5 to 7 minutes to reduce crowding on the 2. These increases were made as part of an $8.9 million package of systemwide service enhancements.[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26)

The Clark Street Tube underwent planned repairs on weekends from June 17, 2017, to June 24, 2018, due to [Hurricane Sandy](/wiki/Hurricane_Sandy "Hurricane Sandy")-related damage. The 3 operated only in Manhattan between Harlem-148th Street and [14th Street](/wiki/14th_Street_(IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line) "14th Street (IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line)") with [4](/wiki/4_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "4 (New York City Subway service)") trains providing service to New Lots Avenue in Brooklyn.[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28)

On November 17, 2019, the MTA made adjustments to weekday evening 3, 4, and 5 service in order to accommodate planned subway work. Late night 3 service to Times Square started an hour earlier, at 10:30 p.m. instead of 11:30 p.m.; to replace 3 service, 4 service was extended to New Lots Avenue. This change, which was approved by the MTA Board on June 27, 2019, was expected to save the agency $900,000 annually.[[29]](#cite_note-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30)

In January 2025, overnight 3 service was quietly extended from Times Square–42nd Street to [34th Street–Penn Station](/wiki/34th_Street%E2%80%93Penn_Station_(IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line) "34th Street–Penn Station (IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line)"), although a few northbound trips continued to enter service at Times Square.[[31]](#cite_note-31) One month later, some weekday early morning and late evening trips were rerouted to and from [137th Street–City College](/wiki/137th_Street%E2%80%93City_College_(IRT_Broadway%E2%80%93Seventh_Avenue_Line) "137th Street–City College (IRT Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line)") as a result of accessibility improvements taking place at 148th Street. Some trips also began ending at [135th Street](/wiki/135th_Street_station_(IRT_Lenox_Avenue_Line) "135th Street station (IRT Lenox Avenue Line)") during the evening.[[32]](#cite_note-32) Effective June 8, 2025, 3 service to 137th Street was discontinued.[[33]](#cite_note-33) One trip to 137th Street-City College was later restored on February 3, 2026. This trip returns back to New Lots Avenue the next weekday morning, starting from 96th Street. After February 18th, 2026, the 137th Street trip was once again discontinued.

In May 2025, as part of the 2025–2029 Capital Program, the MTA proposed that the 3 train be rerouted from New Lots Avenue to [Flatbush Avenue](/wiki/Flatbush_Avenue%E2%80%93Brooklyn_College_station "Flatbush Avenue–Brooklyn College station") at its southern end, as it did prior to 1983. A new 8 service, operating along the Broadway–Seventh Avenue Line, would take over the southernmost portion of the 3's route from Franklin Avenue to New Lots Avenue.[[34]](#cite_note-34)[[35]](#cite_note-Streetsblog_t017-35) The proposal, first suggested in 2023 as part of the MTA's 20-year needs assessment, would remove a bottleneck at the Rogers Avenue Junction south of Franklin Avenue, where 5 express and 3 local trains must cross over each other's tracks.[[35]](#cite_note-Streetsblog_t017-35)
